classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Unit covering rational and irrational numbers, density proofs, decimal representations, and square root spirals.
Basic introduction defining rational numbers as quotients of integers where the denominator is non-zero.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Representation of rational numbers on the number line
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Methods to locate and represent specific rational values accurately along a continuous number line.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Density of rational numbers and its proof
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Proof demonstrating that between any two distinct rational numbers, there exists an infinite number of other rationals.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Finding rational numbers between any two rational numbers
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Algorithmic approaches to identify specific rational values situated strictly between given endpoints.
Analysis of terminating and non-terminating recurring decimal expansions for all rational numbers.
Definition of numbers that cannot be expressed as a ratio p/q and have non-terminating non-recurring decimals.
Rigorous proof by contradiction establishing that the square roots of prime numbers are not rational.
Geometric construction method to visually represent and locate irrational numbers like √n on a plane.
Unit covering polynomials, sequences, algebraic identities, and linear equations in two variables.
Definition of polynomials including degree, terms, coefficients, and classification by number of terms.
Study of arithmetic and geometric progressions including nth term formulas and sum calculations.
Derivation and application of standard algebraic identities using geometric models and factorisation techniques.
Graphical representation of linear equations, slope-intercept form, and methods for solving systems.
Unit covering the Cartesian system, distance formula, midpoint calculations, and historical context.
Overview of the development of coordinate systems from ancient civilizations to modern applications.
Explanation of axes, quadrants, and plotting points using ordered pairs (x, y) on a grid.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Distance between two points in the 2-D plane
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Derivation and application of the distance formula to calculate lengths between any two coordinates.
Formula and method for finding the exact center point between two given coordinates.
Unit covering Euclid's axioms, lines and angles, triangle congruence theorems, quadrilaterals, and circles.

classified_blocks.source_heading: Introduction to Euclid's Geometry: Axioms and Postulates
classified_blocks.source_text_excerpt: Study of the foundational axioms, postulates, definitions, and historical context from India, Egypt, and Greece.
Properties of linear pairs, vertically opposite angles, parallel lines, transversals, and angle sums.
Detailed study of SAS, SSS, ASA, RHS, and AAS congruence conditions with proofs by contradiction.
Properties of parallelograms, trapeziums, and other quadrilaterals including midpoint theorems.
Definitions of chords and arcs, angle properties, cyclic quadrilaterals, and circumcircle constructions.
Unit covering perimeter, area of plane figures including Heron's formula, and surface areas/volumes of solids.
Formulas for perimeter and area of triangles, rectangles, parallelograms, circles, and historical approximations.
Formulas for surface area and volume of cubes, cuboids, cylinders, cones, pyramids, spheres, and hemispheres.
Unit covering data representation, measures of central tendency including weighted averages, and probability concepts.
Techniques for visualizing data using bar graphs and stacked bar graphs to answer investigative questions.
Calculation and interpretation of mean, median, mode, and weighted averages for data sets.
Distinction between empirical and theoretical probability using sample spaces, events, and tree diagrams.
